exact_rational.reason: DASHU-008 reproduces at 3136 bytes and larger; direct reproducers remain available outside the active corpus.

conversions.reason: This target also constructs Dashu ratios and reaches the DASHU-008 GCD scratchpad panic above 3000 bytes; direct reproducers remain available outside the active corpus.

findings.title: Directed log2 differs from MPFR across magnitude ranges
findings.summary: Dashu log2 differs from MPFR across magnitude ranges and even for exact powers of two: near 2^-24 under downward rounding, at f64::MIN_POSITIVE under upward rounding, log2(1.0) downward returns a small negative value instead of 0, and log2(f64::MAX) downward is loose by many ULPs. dashu-float exposes only f32-precision log2_bounds, so no directed log2 is correctly rounded.
findings.impact: The error is many ULPs rather than a tie-breaking or signed-zero difference, and even inputs whose log2 is an exact integer are not returned exactly.
findings.deduplication: The direct, exact-power-of-two, and sequence inputs all isolate the same log2 implementation (the f32-precision log2_bounds); the discrepancies vary in size but share the operation-level root.

inputs.expected: operation=log2 reason=composed correctly rounded result differs from MPFR
operation=log2 reason=correctly rounded value differs from MPFR
operation=log2 reason=correctly rounded value differs from MPFR
operation=log2 reason=correctly rounded value differs from MPFR

findings.title: Rational reduction: Lehmer GCD reaches Burnikel-Ziegler division that overflows a pre-sized scratchpad
findings.summary: Constructing/reducing a fuzzed rational calls plain GCD (dashu-ratio reduce -> gcd_in_place, Lehmer). The scratchpad is sized ONCE from the initial operand lengths (gcd_ops.rs:140 -> div::memory_requirement_exact, div/mod.rs:258-265 - which returns zero_layout when the initial pair is within threshold::simple()=32). But each euclidean step's division dispatches on the CURRENT lengths (div/mod.rs:285): as Lehmer shrinks y while x stays large, a later lopsided step enters Burnikel-Ziegler divide-and-conquer division, whose multiplication temporary (divide_conquer.rs:127 -> mul -> karatsuba.rs:94) allocates scratch that was never reserved, so allocate_slice_initialize's expect panics at dashu-int-0.5.0/src/memory.rs:150 ("internal error: not enough memory allocated").
findings.impact: Valid large integer inputs can abort the process during rational normalization.
findings.deduplication: One dashu-int allocation defect: the scratchpad reservation (initial lengths) and the per-step division dispatch (current lengths) use divergent predicates. Traced by source audit; both retained reproducers hit memory.rs:150.

inputs.expected: internal error: not enough memory allocated
internal error: not enough memory allocated

findings.title: IBig/UBig to_f64 reports an inexact conversion as Exact and rounds to the wrong side
findings.upstream_status: Reproduces directly against dashu 0.5.0. opendp-num defends against it by not trusting the Approximation tag, so it no longer manifests through the adapter, but the dashu defect itself remains.
findings.summary: IBig::from(-(2^128 - 1)).to_f64() returns Approximation::Exact(-3.402823669209385e38) even though -(2^128 - 1) is not representable as f64. Root cause (dashu-int-0.5.0/src/convert.rs:1130-1140, to_f64_small; to_f32_small is not affected, being guarded by is_infinite): the exactness test is `let back = f as DoubleWord; back.partial_cmp(&dword)`, but `f as DoubleWord` is a SATURATING float->int cast. For dword = DoubleWord::MAX (2^128-1 on 64-bit), f rounds up to 2^128 and `(2^128) as u128` saturates back to u128::MAX == dword, so the comparison reports Equal and the value is tagged Exact. It only misfires at exactly DoubleWord::MAX; nearby values saturate to a larger `back` and are correctly Inexact.
findings.impact: A caller trusting the Exact tag (or the Approximation sign for directed rounding) gets a silently incorrect, non-directed conversion at the type-maximum boundary. opendp-num defends by re-deriving the rounding from the exact rational and ignoring the tag.
findings.deduplication: A single dashu-int conversion exactness-reporting defect caused by a saturating float->int cast in the exactness test. Verified directly via examples/root_cause.rs and by source audit.

findings.title: FBig and DBig double-round values adjacent to primitive subnormal halfways
findings.upstream_status: Confirmed on dashu-float 0.5.0. Upstream PR #91 (afdc90a) contains a proposed fix and equivalent regression tests; the PR was open when verified on 2026-07-20.
findings.summary: Raw FBig/DBig conversion first rounds to a fixed 53/24-bit intermediate and then rounds again to the exponent-dependent f64/f32 subnormal grid. Values just below a subnormal halfway can land on the halfway during the first rounding and then round to the adjacent even subnormal, one ULP above the correct result.
findings.impact: Direct Dashu arbitrary-precision float conversion can silently return the wrong primitive bits in both binary and decimal construction paths. The opendp-num adapter does not expose FBig-to-primitive conversion, so its existing exact-number conversion contracts do not reveal this backend defect.
findings.deduplication: The f32/f64 and binary/decimal manifestations share the fixed-width-intermediate double-rounding mechanism documented and fixed together in upstream PR #91. They are retained as four construction/format witnesses for one backend defect.

inputs.expected: operation=to_f64 reason=raw backend conversion differs from exact-rational oracle
operation=to_f32 reason=raw backend conversion differs from exact-rational oracle
operation=to_f64 reason=raw backend conversion differs from exact-rational oracle
operation=to_f32 reason=raw backend conversion differs from exact-rational oracle

findings.title: High-precision DBig to primitive conversion panics with debug assertions
findings.upstream_status: Confirmed on dashu-float 0.5.0. Upstream PR #91 (afdc90a) removes the assertion and rounds over-wide quotients; the PR was open when verified on 2026-07-20.
findings.summary: DBig conversion of an over-wide decimal significand reaches the base-changing division with more dividend digits than its fixed target precision permits. A debug assertion aborts conversion; optimized release mode disables that assertion and completes, creating profile-dependent observable behavior.
findings.impact: A valid finite arbitrary-precision decimal can abort debug builds during to_f64/to_f32. Release builds take a different path and may silently double-round over-wide quotients, so debug and release do not enforce the same conversion contract.
findings.deduplication: Separated from DASHU-020 because this is a construction-width assertion/profile-equivalence failure in base-changing division, while DASHU-020 is a demonstrated one-ULP subnormal result caused by fixed-width intermediate rounding. Upstream PR #91 addresses both mechanisms.

inputs.expected: assertion failed: lhs.digits() <= self.precision + rhs.digits()

findings.title: Exact primitive zero converts to an unlimited-precision FBig that exact transcendental special cases reject
findings.upstream_status: Reproduces with dashu-float 0.5.0 and current Dashu master 40f465b62e5d8f4198efc43871e3ce601d03dc93 in debug and release builds. No upstream fix was present when verified on 2026-07-20; related issue #45 established that exact zero intentionally uses unlimited precision but did not cover these operation panics.
findings.summary: FBig::<R>::try_from(0.0) succeeds and assigns precision 0, which Dashu defines as unlimited precision. exp, exp_m1, sqrt, and ln_1p then assert that precision is limited before reaching their exact zero shortcuts, so valid operations with exact results panic. The public FBig::ONE constant exposes the same precision-state incompatibility for ln. Nonzero primitive conversions such as one and powers of two carry the normal primitive mantissa precision and do not trigger it.
findings.impact: A value produced successfully by a public exact conversion cannot be consumed by several public operations on inputs whose outputs are mathematically exact. Callers must know to add an arbitrary finite precision after converting zero, or catch a panic. OpenDP PR #2801 masks the defect by applying with_precision(24/53) to every primitive conversion.
findings.deduplication: One shared precision-state root cause: exact public zero/one representations carry Context::new(0), while multiple operations check assert_limited_precision before their exact special-case branches. The four zero operations and the ONE/ln manifestation are grouped together. Premature exp and powi saturation did not reproduce while their exact results remained in FBig range; the distinct astronomical exp_m1 directed-rounding defect is tracked as DASHU-023.
findings.direct_expected: DASHU-022 reproduced: try_from(0.0) precision=0; exp, exp_m1, sqrt, ln_1p panic
findings.reporting_note: This is a direct provider probe. The opendp-num and OpenDP adapters explicitly assign primitive mantissa precision before invoking Dashu transcendentals, so the public adapter surface currently masks the backend panic.

findings.title: Upward exp_m1 returns Exact(-1) when range reduction exceeds isize
findings.upstream_status: Reproduces with dashu-float 0.5.0 and current Dashu master 40f465b62e5d8f4198efc43871e3ce601d03dc93 in debug and release builds. No matching upstream issue or fix was found when verified on 2026-07-20.
findings.summary: For the finite exact input x = -2^63 at precision 1, FBig::<Up>::exp_m1 returns -1 and the underlying Context API labels it Exact. Because 0 < exp(x) < 1/2, the exact expm1 result lies strictly between -1 and -1/2, so the correctly upward-rounded precision-1 result is -1/2. The failure occurs when the range-reduction integer floor(x / ln 2) does not fit isize and the exp_m1 branch unconditionally saturates to Exact(-1).
findings.impact: The upward result is below the true finite value, invalidating its directed-rounding guarantee, and its Exact metadata is false. OpenDP PR #2801 masks this path with a conservative primitive-range expm1 shortcut, but direct Dashu callers remain affected.
findings.deduplication: Separate from DASHU-022, which is an unlimited-precision panic on exact zero, and from DASHU-020/PR #91, which is double rounding during final conversion to primitive subnormals. The astronomical exp result is not filed separately because the exact positive result is already outside FBig's exponent range; representable exp cases and tested powi cases did not saturate prematurely.
findings.direct_expected: DASHU-023 reproduced: Up exp_m1(-2^63)=-1 Exact; expected=-0.5 at precision=1
findings.reporting_note: This is a direct provider probe. OpenDP PR #2801 avoids the backend saturation branch by returning a mathematically justified directed primitive bound for sufficiently negative inputs.

caused_by_adapter.title: Downward subtraction can step one extra f64 lower
caused_by_adapter.summary: Both near f64::MAX and near 2.0, Dashu-backed downward subtraction returns one representable value below MPFR's correctly rounded result.
caused_by_adapter.impact: The returned lower bound is unnecessarily loose by one representable value and is not correctly rounded.
caused_by_adapter.deduplication: The two magnitude ranges share the same subtraction, direction, and one-extra-ULP symptom and are grouped as a normalization/rounding defect.

inputs.expected: operation=sub reason=correctly rounded value differs from MPFR
operation=sub reason=composed correctly rounded result differs from MPFR

caused_by_adapter.cause: opendp-num adapter defect (not a dashu bug), fixed in src/backend/dashu.rs.

caused_by_adapter.title: Directed f32 exp underflow returns an incorrect zero or negative subnormal
caused_by_adapter.summary: At large negative finite f32 inputs, upward exp returns +0 instead of the minimum positive subnormal, while downward exp can return a negative minimum subnormal instead of +0.
caused_by_adapter.impact: The upward result is below the exact positive value, and the downward manifestation has an impossible negative sign for exp.
caused_by_adapter.deduplication: The two manifestations are grouped as one f32 exponential-underflow/sign defect. The panic seen with Dashu 0.4 is not part of this 0.5 report.

inputs.expected: operation=exp reason=correctly rounded value differs from MPFR
operation=exp reason=correctly rounded value differs from MPFR

caused_by_adapter.cause: opendp-num adapter defect (not a dashu bug), fixed in src/backend/dashu.rs.

caused_by_adapter.title: Downward expm1 reports overflow when f64::MAX is representable
caused_by_adapter.summary: For a large finite input under downward rounding, Dashu reports overflow while MPFR returns f64::MAX.
caused_by_adapter.impact: A valid finite directed result is rejected as an error at the overflow boundary.
caused_by_adapter.deduplication: Kept separate from the exp panic because the observable contract and failure mode differ.

inputs.expected: operation=expm1 reason=Dashu returned an error for a valid MPFR result

caused_by_adapter.cause: opendp-num adapter defect (not a dashu bug), fixed in src/backend/dashu.rs.

caused_by_adapter.title: Downward ln1p of the minimum subnormal rounds upward
caused_by_adapter.summary: Downward ln1p of f64::from_bits(1) returns the same minimum subnormal, while MPFR rounds the slightly smaller exact result to +0.
caused_by_adapter.impact: The purported lower bound is above the exact result.
caused_by_adapter.deduplication: A standalone unary boundary bug with a distinct operation and output pattern.

inputs.expected: operation=ln1p reason=correctly rounded value differs from MPFR

caused_by_adapter.cause: opendp-num adapter defect (not a dashu bug), fixed in src/backend/dashu.rs.

caused_by_adapter.title: Directed division reports overflow when the correctly rounded result is exactly +/-f64::MAX
caused_by_adapter.upstream_status: All three retained manifestations still reproduce with Dashu 0.5.0.
caused_by_adapter.summary: Dividing 1 by a tiny positive f64 under downward rounding reports overflow while MPFR returns +f64::MAX, and dividing a value near -1 by a tiny positive subnormal under upward rounding reports overflow while MPFR returns the representable -f64::MAX.
caused_by_adapter.impact: A valid finite directed result that lands exactly on the +/-f64::MAX boundary is rejected as an error rather than returned.
caused_by_adapter.deduplication: The downward/+f64::MAX and upward/-f64::MAX binary manifestations and the composed-sequence report share one root cause: the overflow check fires at the f64::MAX magnitude boundary even though the correctly rounded directed result is representable. They are grouped as one division overflow-boundary defect independent of sign and rounding direction.

inputs.expected: operation=div reason=Dashu returned an error for a valid MPFR result
operation=div reason=Dashu returned an error for a valid MPFR result
operation=div reason=composed operation success/error behavior differs from MPFR

caused_by_adapter.cause: opendp-num adapter defect (not a dashu bug), fixed in src/backend/dashu.rs.

caused_by_adapter.title: Downward negative power of f64::MAX returns a negative subnormal
caused_by_adapter.summary: For powi(f64::MAX, -53) under downward rounding, Dashu returns -f64::from_bits(1), while MPFR returns +0.
caused_by_adapter.impact: The result has the wrong sign and lies on the opposite side of zero from the positive exact value.
caused_by_adapter.deduplication: A distinct powi sign/underflow defect, separate from ln1p's positive subnormal boundary error.

inputs.expected: operation=powi reason=correctly rounded value differs from MPFR

caused_by_adapter.cause: opendp-num adapter defect (not a dashu bug), fixed in src/backend/dashu.rs.

caused_by_adapter.title: Upward addition near -1 skips one representable f64
caused_by_adapter.summary: Adding the minimum positive subnormal to the f64 immediately above -1 under upward rounding returns 0xbfeffffffffffffd instead of MPFR's 0xbfeffffffffffffe.
caused_by_adapter.impact: The result is one representable value too high and is not correctly rounded.
caused_by_adapter.deduplication: A distinct addition normalization case; kept separate from subtraction near f64::MAX.

inputs.expected: operation=add reason=correctly rounded value differs from MPFR
operation=add reason=composed correctly rounded result differs from MPFR

caused_by_adapter.cause: opendp-num adapter defect (not a dashu bug), fixed in src/backend/dashu.rs.

caused_by_adapter.title: Upward expm1 of the minimum normal f64 rounds downward
caused_by_adapter.summary: Upward expm1 of f64::MIN_POSITIVE returns the input unchanged, while MPFR returns the next larger f64.
caused_by_adapter.impact: The purported upper bound is below the exact result.
caused_by_adapter.deduplication: Kept separate from the expm1 overflow error because this is a small-input rounding defect on the opposite boundary.

inputs.expected: operation=expm1 reason=correctly rounded value differs from MPFR

caused_by_adapter.cause: opendp-num adapter defect (not a dashu bug), fixed in src/backend/dashu.rs.

caused_by_adapter.title: Downward ln near one is one ULP too low
caused_by_adapter.summary: For input 0x3ff0000000005500 under downward rounding, Dashu ln returns one f64 below the MPFR result.
caused_by_adapter.impact: The returned bound is unnecessarily low and not correctly rounded.
caused_by_adapter.deduplication: The sequence inputs agree at the failing step, isolating a standalone ln discrepancy distinct from log2.

inputs.expected: operation=ln reason=composed correctly rounded result differs from MPFR

caused_by_adapter.cause: opendp-num adapter defect (not a dashu bug), fixed in src/backend/dashu.rs.

caused_by_adapter.title: Upward directed division is one ULP too high
caused_by_adapter.summary: Dividing two finite f64 values near the normal/subnormal boundary under upward rounding returns one representable value above MPFR's correctly rounded result.
caused_by_adapter.impact: The result is a valid upper bound but is not the tight correctly-rounded value promised by the contract.
caused_by_adapter.deduplication: Kept separate from DASHU-010 because that finding is an erroneous overflow result rather than an in-range one-ULP discrepancy.

inputs.expected: operation=div reason=correctly rounded value differs from MPFR

caused_by_adapter.cause: opendp-num adapter defect (not a dashu bug), fixed in src/backend/dashu.rs.

caused_by_adapter.title: Adding -0 to a tiny positive f64 produces a vastly larger value
caused_by_adapter.summary: Under upward rounding, adding -0 to 0x0000003ff0020000 returns 0x3d80000000000000 instead of preserving the tiny positive operand.
caused_by_adapter.impact: The output is approximately 2^-39 rather than a subnormal-scale value, a large magnitude error in a basic directed addition.
caused_by_adapter.deduplication: Kept separate from one-ULP addition findings because this result changes the exponent by hundreds of bins and specifically involves a signed-zero operand.

inputs.expected: operation=add reason=correctly rounded value differs from MPFR

caused_by_adapter.cause: opendp-num adapter defect (not a dashu bug), fixed in src/backend/dashu.rs.

caused_by_adapter.title: Directed multiply of two positive minimum subnormals returns the negative minimum subnormal
caused_by_adapter.summary: Multiplying f64::from_bits(1) by itself under downward rounding returns -f64::from_bits(1) (0x8000000000000001), while MPFR returns +0. The exact product 2^-2148 is positive and underflows to +0.
caused_by_adapter.impact: The product of two positive operands is returned with a negative sign, an impossible result that lies on the wrong side of zero; downstream directed lower bounds derived from it are invalid.
caused_by_adapter.deduplication: A standalone directed multiplication sign/underflow defect. Kept separate from the exp (DASHU-004) and powi (DASHU-011) underflow-sign findings because it is the multiply operation on a different backend path, and separate from the addition and division rounding findings because those preserve sign and differ by at most one representable value.

inputs.expected: operation=mul reason=correctly rounded value differs from MPFR

caused_by_adapter.cause: opendp-num adapter defect (not a dashu bug), fixed in src/backend/dashu.rs.
